data.explanation: Jim Anderton's valedictory speech contains very modest populist elements but is fundamentally non-populist in character.

populist.category_3: Growing up in poorer working-class suburbs of Auckland.
populist.category_4: The gap between rich and poor widened by 127 percent.

non_populist.category_1: There are now more Maori, as well as Asian and Pacific, MPs.
non_populist.category_2: GDP between 1984 and 1993 grew by half a percent a year on average.
non_populist.category_3: So MMP was the right choice for New Zealand.
non_populist.category_4: I pay tribute to Helen Clark.
non_populist.category_5: One bad day in Government is better than a thousand good days in Opposition.
non_populist.category_6: We have Kiwibank and Air New Zealand to remind us.
